data.description: The Luma calendar embed/fetch is failing with 404s. Network log shows: GET https://api.luma.com/calendar/get?api_id=nyc-tech -> 404 and GET https://api.luma.com/calendar/get-items?calendar_api_id=nyc-tech&pagination_limit=10&period=future -> 404. The calendar_api_id 'nyc-tech' is either wrong, renamed, or the calendar was deleted on Luma's side. Scope: (1) confirm the current correct Luma calendar API ID for Fractal NYC events (likely needs human/Crystal to verify in Luma admin); (2) update wherever the ID is hardcoded — likely an env var, a constant in src/lib/luma.* or a config file, or directly in a component; (3) verify events render correctly after the change. There are also Sentry 403s in the console (o370968.ingest.us.sentry.io ... 403) — likely a separate auth/DSN issue, note in comments but treat as out of scope unless trivially related. Likely needs_human at planning time to confirm the correct calendar ID. Acceptance: calendar loads events with no 404s in the console.
data.title: Luma calendar 404 — calendar_api_id 'nyc-tech' no longer resolves

data.body:
Public Luma calendar URL: https://luma.com/nyc-tech — slug 'nyc-tech' is still live and correct on Luma's side.

The 404 is from the API call (api.luma.com/calendar/get?api_id=nyc-tech), which suggests Luma's API no longer accepts the public slug as the api_id. Luma's API typically expects a 'cal-XXXXXXXX' style identifier instead. Next step at planning: pull the actual calendar API ID from Luma admin (Crystal can grab it from the calendar's settings/embed page) and replace the 'nyc-tech' value in our code. Likely needs_human briefly to retrieve the real cal-XXX ID.

Attempted to scrape the cal-XXX calendar API ID from the public Luma page — unsuccessful.

Attempts:
1. WebFetch https://luma.com/nyc-tech with prompt to find cal- prefixed strings, api_id fields, or UUIDs in __NEXT_DATA__ or script blocks → no matches (WebFetch strips script tags before content extraction).
2. WebFetch https://api.luma.com/calendar/get?slug=nyc-tech → HTTP 400.
3. WebFetch https://api.luma.com/calendar/get?calendar_api_id=cal-nyc-tech → HTTP 400.

Conclusion: the cal-XXX ID cannot be retrieved via headless scraping with the tools available. Recommend escalating to needs_human — Crystal (or whoever has Luma admin for the nyc-tech calendar) can grab the actual calendar API ID from Luma's admin interface (typically under calendar settings / embed code / 'connect via API'). Once we have it, the fix is a one-line config change wherever 'nyc-tech' is currently used as the api_id.

Need: the real cal-XXX calendar API ID for the Fractal Campus NYC Luma calendar (public slug nyc-tech). Crystal or whoever has Luma admin can grab it from the calendar's settings/embed-code page on Luma.
